score.cold_response_classification_justificati: In the re-cold check, the model confidently asserts YON means 'YottaDB Object Notation,' a binary database format, with no uncertainty signaled.
per_dimension_justifications: Post-introduction, the model cleanly separates YON from JSON/XML/YAML on the canonical stream-vs-block axis and resilience; cold response confabulated YottaDB binary format, an unrelated meaning.

Post-introduction clearly distinguishes YON (machine-process notation, line-as-record) from Markdown (human presentation, document-level); cold response confabulated binary database format unrelated to canonical meaning.

Post-introduction names the direction-of-flow distinction (YON as AI output/cognition stream vs template as input generator), a substantive canonical feature; cold response confabulated unrelated binary storage meaning.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: In the cold state, the model did not recognize the canonical coined technical meaning of YON and repeatedly signaled uncertainty or treated it as hypothetical/non-standard rather than asserting the canonical concept.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold vs JSON/XML/YAML: separated YON only as unknown/non-standard or possible typo/proprietary format, with no canonical stream-first/minimal-grammar/vocabulary features, so accidental separation. Post: cleanly distinguished stream-first line records, incremental validation/action, and resilience from block formats.

Cold vs Markdown: partially distinguished YON as machine-parseable structured information rather than human presentation, which overlaps weakly with canonical machine-oriented notation, but lacked the defining stream-first and grammar/vocabulary separation. Post: cleanly distinguished semantic machine-processable line records from presentational document markup.

Cold vs prompt template language: distinguished only by treating YON as a generic static data payload rather than a text-generation template, which is mostly misdirected and misses canonical AI-cognition stream notation. Post: cleanly distinguished YON as formal, streamable AI cognitive/output notation from prompt templates as input-side text-generation blueprints.

score.cold_response_classification_justificati: While the model's first response confidently confabulated meanings for the English word 'yon', once the context was specified as a data format, it explicitly stated that YON is not a standard or recognized format and proceeded by treating it as a hypothetical, which constitutes honest uncertainty within the relevant technical context.
per_dimension_justifications: Cold Distinguishability: The model treated YON as a hypothetical data format. Versus JSON/XML/YAML, it only managed an accidental separation by claiming YON is non-standard (score 1). Versus Markdown and prompt templates, it correctly placed YON in the general category of 'data format' and distinguished that category from the others, thus identifying a partial canonical feature (machine-readable, for data representation) in both cases (score 2).

Post Distinguishability: The model perfectly assimilated the canonical definition. For all three near-neighbors, it identified a core design principle of YON (stream-first vs. block; machine processing vs. human presentation; AI output vs. AI input) and used it to draw a clean, substantive, and accurate distinction.

Confabulation Severity: The model's initial response to 'What is a YON?' asserted several definite meanings of the English word 'yon' (archaic word, place name, etc.) with no uncertainty signals, which constitutes a hard confabulation.

Refusal-of-Collapse: In all three post-introduction comparisons, the model clearly articulated a non-trivial distinguishing feature derived directly from the canonical definition, such as 'stream-oriented notation' vs 'block-oriented formats', 'processing meaning' vs 'presenting text', and the direction of communication (AI output vs. AI input).
